title: ranunculus circinatus var. rodiei
intro: Ranunculus circinatus var. rodiei, also known as water buttercup, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its alkaloid content. The water buttercup plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, as well as possessing antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
